channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Mardle: Menzies' greatest moment - he knows he can handle anything now
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: FIFA probes post-match scuffle after Spain’s World Cup final win over Argentina
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FIFA has launched an inquiry into a post-match scuffle between Spanish and Argentine players after Spain’s World Cup final victory. Learn more about the in
Read More: https://punchng.com/fifa-probes-post-match-scuffle-after-spains-world-cup-final-win-over-argentina/

item.title: Papers: Man Utd looking at Hall, Robinson to strengthen left-back options
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The top stories and transfer rumours from Tuesday's newspapers...

item.title: Why are gasoline prices rising faster than oil prices? Blame it on the ‘crack.’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The spread between the cost of barrels of gasoline and crude oil — known as the “crack spread” — has widened sharply.

item.title: STAC Marine strengthens brand after $20m Abo FPSO acquisition
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Co-Founder and Creative Director of Flip Visuals, Austine Nwaojei, said STAC Marine understood from the outset the importance of establishing a strong corporate identity as it expanded its operations.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As FG unveils feasibility study, launches ICAO-backed business plan for aviation decarbonisation The Nigerian government on Monday took a major step toward decarbonising its aviation sector with the inauguration of a National High-Level Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Steering Committee and t

item.title: Menzies shocks world No 2 Humphries in World Matchplay thriller
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Cameron Menzies produced a massive upset to defeat 2024 champion Luke Humphries 10-7 at the World Matchplay on Monday night.

item.title: Visa fraud: Immigration boss not under probe – EFCC
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The EFCC has clarified that Nigeria Immigration Service CG, Kemi Nandap, is not under investigation for visa fraud, despite ongoing probes into other offic
Read More: https://punchng.com/visa-fraud-immigration-boss-not-under-probe-efcc/

item.title: Chaos in India as ‘Cockroach activists’ march on parliament
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Chaos outside India's parliament as protesters tried to storm it; satirical Cockroach Janta Party gains traction.

item.title: HUGE SHOCK! Humphries OUT after Big Fish finish from Menzies!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of the first-round clash between Luke Humphries and Cameron Menzies at the World Matchplay.

item.title: Man Utd and Arsenal fight over Kone - Tuesday's gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Manchester United and Arsenal fight over France's Manu Kone, Nico Williams could be heading to the Premier League champions, and West Ham reject latest Crysencio Summerville offer.

item.title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions improve.

item.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.

item.title: New UK PM Burnham stamps authority with cabinet shakeup
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The monarch's fourth prime minister since he acceded to the throne in 2022, and Britain's seventh in just over a decade, Burnham said the country needed to show the world it could "regain stability". The post New UK PM Burnham stamps authority with cabinet shakeup appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Gombe SSG warns civil servants against absenteeism

Gombe State SSG, Ibrahim Njodi, warns civil servants at the Kaduna Liaison Office against absenteeism and poor work ethics, stressing zero tolerance for in

item.title: I will claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

item.title: Insecurity: Osun ALGON Suspends Transport Unions Activites In Motor Parks
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Association of Local Governments of Nigeria (ALGON), Osun State chapter, has announced the immediate suspension of the activities of all transport unions operating in public motor parks across the state, citing worsening insecurity and recurring violence linked to the leadership crisis within th

item.title: Donald Trump to impose 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, White House says
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Turmoil likely as Trump officials say Canada unfairly discriminated against US autos, alcohol and dairy productsDonald Trump is imposing 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods in response to the country retaliating against previous US tariffs, the White House announced Monday, declaring Canada has unfai

item.title: War on Iran: War resumes 30 days after MoU
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: 30 days into the US-Iran MoU the worst fighting in months has erupted threatening to unravel the ceasefire.

item.title: Trump imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has announced 26 new appointments into 10 federal government agencies and commissions, with former governor of Ekiti State, Ayo Fayose, emerging as chairman of the Rural Electrification Agency (REA), and Major General Junaid Bindawa as chairman of the National Salaries, I

item.title: Gov Sule Inaugurates N4.401bn 12km Agwada-Udege Road
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nasrawa State Governor, Abdullahi Sule, on Monday inaugurated the N4.401bn 12.4-km Agwada-Udege road in Kokona Local Government Area of the state. Speaking during the event on Monday, Governor Sule said the construction of the road was a deliberate effort of his administration to link rural communit

item.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Why cheap Chinese AI models could actually be a boon for Nvidia, Micron and other chip stocks
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Moonshot AI’s Kimi K3 could encourage a surge in enterprise workloads and provide a long-term tailwind to chip demand.

item.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New York court on

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC), Sokoto State Chapter, has announced the nomination of Hon. Bello Isah Ambarura, a member representing Illela/Gwadabawa Federal Constituency in the House of Representatives, as the Deputy Governorship candidate to Hon. Manir Muhammad Dan-Iya, the party’s Governo

item.title: Federal Govrtnment Revives 16 Abandoned E-library Projects In Unity Colleges
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government said it has revived 16 abandoned digital e-library projects across Federal Unity Colleges and commissioned a state-of-the-art digital hub at the federal government College, Kano, as part of efforts to modernise public schools and expand access to technology-driven learning. Th

item.title: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Last October, we told you how the FCC had given itself the power to retroactively ban gadgets that have already received its approval to be imported and sold in the United States. Now, the FCC's getting ready to wield that power for the first time, by cracking down on the "DJI front companies" suspe

item.title: Ovaloop Technologies Launches Retail Operating System for African SMEs
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: | By: Francis Onyemachi Ovaloop Technologies has unveiled a retail operating system designed to help retailers across Nigeria and Africa manage inventory, payments, sales and business reporting from a single platform. The company said the expansion of Nigeria’s digital payment ecosystem h

item.title: We’ll talk to everybody, including terrorists, to end insecurity — Kwankwaso

item.description: No fewer than 143 indigenous and international oil and gas companies would on Tuesday, battle for 50 oil and gas blocks as the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum Regulatory Commission (NUPRC) begins the commercial bid phase of the country’s 2025 Licensing Round in Abuja. item.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil held a two-day gain as the US and Iran continued hostilities in the Middle East, while Yemen’s Houthi militants threatened to blockade Saudi Arabia’s maritime traffic in the Red Sea.

item.title: Conflict Rattles Stocks, Bonds as Oil Holds Gains: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil held near its highest close since mid-June — weighing on stocks and bonds — as an escalation in Middle East hostilities outweighed attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

item.title: Sanwo-Olu Pays N3bn Compensation To 331 Lagos Property Owners Displaced By Road Project
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Lagos State governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, on Monday presented compensation cheques worth ₦3 billion to 331 persons whose land and property were acquired for strategic road infrastructure projects across the state. In his address during the presentation ceremony held at the Adeyemi Bero Auditorium, A

item.title: Bauchi Reforms BAROTA, Moves Staff To Mainstream Civil Service
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: In a major reform of the Bauchi Road Traffic Agency (BAROTA), Governor Bala Abdulkadir Mohammed has approved the transfer of the agency’s workforce into the Bauchi State Civil Service. The decision, announced by the Commissioner for Works and Transport, Prof. Titus Ketkuka, is expected to impr

item.title: Argentina squad returns, without Messi, after World Cup final loss
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Most of Argentina's football team landed back home Monday, as thousands of fans waited to welcome them, following their 1-0 loss to Spain in the World Cup final, according to the airports authority. item.title: Nigeria’s Envoy to Portugal Presents Letter of Credence
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nigeria’s Ambassador-Designate to Portugal, Erelu Angela Adebayo, has taken a major step towards commencing her diplomatic assignment with the presentation of copies of her letters of credence to Portuguese authorities. Adebayo presented the documents to Ambassador Francisco Ribeiro Telles, Secretar

item.title: Millions Celebrate As Spain Parade World Cup Trophy Through Madrid
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The squad and coach Luis de la Fuente were introduced one by one to the fans, each with their own choice of music that they sung along to, wearing "We are champions" shirts. A statement by the Army Spokesperson Col. Appolonia Anele said the advertisement “is false, fraud

item.title: Russia, North Korea accuse US, allies of regional provocation
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Russia and North Korea have criticised the United States and its allies for the expansion of “provocative military activities” around the Korean Peninsula. The exercises have been the main cause of “the deteriorating security situation” in Northeast Asia and beyond, according

item.title: Mustapha Supporters Declare Support For APM In Bauchi
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A group of youths identified as supporters of former Bauchi State Commissioner, Farouk Mustapha, have announced their defection to the Allied Peoples Movement (APM) in the State. The group, who made this announcement on Monday, said they joined the party under the leadership of Bula Bulan Kaura, cit

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The NDLEA Commander confirmed that investigations are ongoing and the suspects will be charged to court soon.

item.title: DSS Vows To Appeal Life Sentence On Ansaru Terror Commanders
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) has described Monday’s life sentence handed to two top commanders of the Ansaru Terror organisation by a Federal High Court in Abuja as “Too light”, hence its decision to appeal against the same. item.title: U.S. hits Canada with stiff new tariffs, escalating trade tensions
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday said it would slap 50% tariffs on some goods imported from Canada, in an effort to end what it sees as discriminatory trade practices by Ottawa against the U.S. automobile, dairy and alcohol industries.

item.description: Tens of thousands of fans greet La Roja as they travel down Madrid's historic avenues to Cibeles Square.

While those scenes were beautifully crafted and tremendously acted, there was something a little bit off about the way

item.title: Kaduna ADC Leaders End Factional Rift After Reconciliation Meeting
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Factional leaders of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Kaduna State have resolved their differences following a reconciliation meeting convened by the party’s 2023 governorship candidate, Hon. Isa Ashiru.   The meeting, which lasted about three hours at Ashiru’s office

item.title: Trump signs order imposing new 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump signed orders on Monday imposing new 50% tariffs on some Canadian goods, citing what the White House called Canada's "discriminatory treatment" of US alcohol, automobiles and dairy products. The tariffs take effect in 30 days, with energy, potash and sector-specific imports

item.title: 2026 World Cup: FIFA opens investigation into Spain vs Argentina final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: FIFA has opened a disciplinary investigation into the aftermath of the 2026 World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. There were ugly scenes at the end of the game, involving players and coaches from both teams. The game was ultimately decided by Ferran Torres’ goal in the 106th minute. Bef

item.title: Police arrest suspected motorcycle thief, recover 7 bikes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ekiti State Police Command has recorded another major breakthrough in its fight against crime with the arrest of one suspected motorcycle thief, two suspected receivers of stolen property, and the recovery of seven motorcycles believed to have been stolen. The arrest was made on Saturday, July 1

item.title: ‘The Pain Is Immense,’ Says Messi After World Cup Final Defeat
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: At the World Cup, he has now lost two finals, in 2014 and 2026, and won one, in 2022, against France. The post ‘The Pain Is Immense,’ Says Messi After World Cup Final Defeat appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Lebanese army deploys to three southern pilot zones under US-backed security framework
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The Lebanese army began assuming security responsibilities in three southern villages on Monday under a US-backed pilot initiative, the State Department said. Operations in Froun, Srifa and Zawtar al-Gharbiya mark the first implementation of a framework aimed at enabling a phased Israeli withdrawal

item.title: Dollar to Naira Exchange Rate Today, July 20 2026: Nigerian Naira extends appreciation Ahead of CBN’s interest rate decision
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The naira appreciated at the official foreign exchange market on Monday ahead of the Central Bank of Nigeria’s, CBN Monetary Policy Committee, MPC, interest rate decision. CBN data showed that the naira gained slightly to N1,380.10 per dollar on Monday from N1,380.18 per dollar recorded last F

item.title: Court Holds Police, Firm Liable For Ex-CP’s Son’s Death, Awards N100m Cost
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal High Court in Lagos has ordered the Police and Exus Pharmaceutical (Nig.) Limited to pay N100 million as damages in connection with the death of the son of retired Commissioner of Police, Omokhinyema Okubo, while in police custody. Justice Ibrahim Ahmad Kala, in a judgment delivered on M

item.title: Albanian protesters pelt lawmakers’ cars over resort projects
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Protesters threw eggs at lawmakers' vehicles outside Albania's parliament, demanding changes to laws.

item.title: Alleged visa racketeering: Immigration CG not under investigation – EFCC
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has denied reports that it is investigating the Comptroller General (CG) of the Nigeria Immigration Service (NIS), Kemi Nandap, over alleged visa racketeering. The post Alleged visa racketeering: Immigration CG not under investigation – EFCC

item.title: AAGP gets major boost as ECOWAS member states sign historic intergovernmental agreement
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The African Atlantic Gas Pipeline (AAGP) Project has received a major boost towards its actualisation as the Heads of State of ECOWAS members... The post AAGP gets major boost as ECOWAS member states sign historic intergovernmental agreement appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: New copyright levy reform to direct 50 percent collections straight to artists, labels
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: A reformed copyright levy is set to allocate 50 percent of collections directly to rights owners, including artists and labels, read more New copyright levy reform to direct 50 percent collections straight to artists, labels

item.title: FIFA investigating on-pitch brawl after Spain vs Argentina World Cup final
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Argentina's Paredes was seen shoving Spanish players, which led to an on-field melee, while Spain's Yamal prayed calmly.

item.title: Global oil prices settle at one-month high despite new Iran ceasefire proposal, as blockade on Saudi Arabia by Yemen’s Houthis keeps market on edge
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices settled at one-month highs in volatile trading on Monday as traders weighed escalating geopolitical tensions following a fresh barrage of strikes across the Middle East over the weekend against reports of a new ceasefire proposal between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Palace seek £55m-plus for Lacroix as talks with Chelsea begin
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Chelsea are in talks with Crystal Palace over the signing of defender Maxence Lacroix.

item.title: Husband, wife nabbed as NDLEA uncovers 412kg drug warehouse in Benin
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “I am a secondary school graduate and a truck driver, but joblessness made a friend introduce me to drug trafficking. My wife and I have twins to cater for and the financial pressure was too much. If given another chance, I will not go back to drugs,” he pleaded. Commander Ofoyeju said the bust unde

item.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US says the attacks were aimed at degrading Iran's capabilities to attack ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Court rules FCCPC can regulate but cannot license airtime providers
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Federal High Court in Lagos on Monday established a clear distinction regarding the regulatory powers of the Federal Competition read more Court rules FCCPC can regulate but cannot license airtime providers

item.title: Trump rejects Mamdani threat to arrest Israel’s Netanyahu for war crimes
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US president insists Netanyahu will not be arrested, despite ICC warrant.
